subtitle.zh: 均衡、频率、阻断与平衡——理论落地训练
subtitle.en: Equilibrium, frequencies, blockers and balance — theory into reps

text.zh: GTO（博弈论最优）是一套不可被剥削的均衡策略：当你按它行动，对手无论怎样偏离都无法长期占你便宜。它不追求对每个具体对手赢得最多，而是先立一条谁也撬不动的基线。
text.en: GTO (game-theory optimal) is an unexploitable equilibrium strategy: play it, and no matter how an opponent deviates, they cannot profit off you in the long run. It does not try to win the most against each specific player — it plants a baseline nobody can lever open.

text.zh: 石头剪刀布的均衡是各出三分之一、完全随机。你可以对着它出一万局石头，期望仍是零——这就是「不可被剥削」的手感。扑克的 GTO 同理，只是树大得多。
text.en: Rock-paper-scissors at equilibrium is one-third each, fully random. Throw rock at it ten thousand times and your expectation stays zero — that is what 'unexploitable' feels like. Poker GTO is the same idea on a vastly bigger tree.

text.zh: 常见错误：把 GTO 当成每一手的万能答案。真实的均衡解极其复杂，本应用提供的是教学近似；而且对着明显的漏洞继续打均衡，等于放着更高的期望不拿。
text.en: Common mistake: treating GTO as the universal answer for every hand. True equilibrium solutions are immensely complex — this app teaches approximations — and playing pure equilibrium into an obvious leak leaves better expectation on the table.

text.zh: 学习顺序：先把基线练进肌肉，再学习何时、向哪个方向偏离。
text.en: Order of learning: drill the baseline into muscle first, then learn when and which way to deviate.

text.zh: 高手不问「他拿的是哪一手」，而问「他的整个范围长什么样」。对手每个动作都在过滤范围；你自己的决策，也应当是替整个范围做的，而不是替这一把的两张牌。
text.en: Strong players do not ask 'which hand does he hold' but 'what does his whole range look like.' Every action filters a range — and your own decisions should be made for your entire range, not for tonight's two cards.

text.zh: UTG 开局加注后，他的「手牌」其实是本应用图表里的 182 个组合：大对子、大同花、强高张。在 K 高面上，这团云密集命中；在 765 连面上，它大片落空。
text.en: After a UTG open, his 'hand' is really the 182 combos in this app's chart: big pairs, big suited cards, strong broadways. On a king-high board that cloud lands densely; on 765 it largely whiffs.

text.zh: 常见错误：单点读牌——「他肯定是 AK」。一旦猜错，后面每条街的推理全部坍塌；范围思维允许你部分正确，而不是全对或全错。
text.en: Common mistake: pinpoint reads — 'he definitely has AK.' Guess wrong once and every later street collapses; range thinking lets you be partly right instead of all-or-nothing.

text.zh: 练习方法：训练答题时先大声（或心里）说出对手范围，再选自己的动作。
text.en: Practice: before answering a drill, say the opponent's range out loud (or silently) — then pick your action.

text.zh: 均衡策略常常不是「这手牌永远做 X」，而是「这手牌 70% 做 X、30% 做 Y」。混合让你的每条线里都同时住着强牌与诈唬，对手无法通过你的动作反推你的牌。
text.en: Equilibrium often is not 'always do X with this hand' but '70% X, 30% Y.' Mixing keeps strong hands and bluffs living together on every line, so opponents cannot reverse-engineer your holding from your action.

text.zh: BTN 面对 CO 开局拿 A5s：有时 3-bet 施压，有时直接弃牌。两种动作都对——重要的是比例，而不是某一次的选择。
text.en: A5s on the BTN facing a CO open: sometimes it 3-bets, sometimes it folds. Both actions are right — what matters is the ratio, not any single instance.

text.zh: 常见错误：把每手牌的打法写死成固定模式。固定模式就是可读模式——观察力好的对手两圈就能把你锁死。
text.en: Common mistake: hard-coding one fixed line per hand. Fixed patterns are readable patterns — an observant opponent locks you down within two orbits.

text.zh: 训练里的「可接受」评级，正是混合策略的另一翼：它不扣满分，因为它在真实均衡里确实出现。
text.en: The 'acceptable' grade in drills is exactly the other wing of a mix: it is not marked fully wrong because it genuinely appears in equilibrium.

text.zh: 你手里的每张牌，都让对手少了若干种拿法——这就是阻断效应。持有一张 A，对手拿 AA 的组合从 6 种降到 3 种、AK 从 16 种降到 12 种；选择诈唬牌时，优先选阻断对方跟注范围的牌。
text.en: Every card you hold removes combinations from the opponent's range — the blocker effect. Holding one ace cuts his AA combos from 6 to 3 and AK from 16 to 12; when choosing bluffs, prefer cards that block his calling range.

text.zh: 牌面三张黑桃，你持 A♠ 却没有同花：这张 A♠ 阻断了坚果同花——对手最强的跟注理由变少了，你的诈唬成功率随之上升。
text.en: Three spades on board and you hold the A♠ without a flush: that A♠ blocks the nut flush — his best reason to call just got rarer, and your bluff succeeds more often.

text.zh: 常见错误：高估阻断、忽略基本面。阻断是天平上的砝码，不是整座天平；一手毫无后路的烂牌，不会因为阻断了什么就变成好诈唬。
text.en: Common mistake: overrating blockers while ignoring fundamentals. Blockers are weights on the scale, not the scale itself; a hopeless hand with no backup does not become a good bluff just because it blocks something.

text.zh: 记两个经典：A♠ 在三黑桃面是诈唬加分项；持 A 让 3-bet 诈唬更不容易撞上 AA/AK。
text.en: Two classics to remember: the A♠ on three-spade boards upgrades bluffs, and holding an ace keeps your 3-bet bluffs from running into AA/AK.

title.zh: 平衡：价值与诈唬的比例

text.zh: 河牌下注的均衡比例由尺寸决定：下注越大，给对手的赔率越差，你的范围里就可以（也应该）混入越多诈唬。底池大小的下注，均衡诈唬占比约三分之一。
text.en: The equilibrium ratio on river bets follows from sizing: the bigger the bet, the worse the price you offer, and the more bluffs your range can (and should) carry. A pot-sized bet balances at roughly one-third bluffs.

text.zh: 你在河牌下注一个底池：对手跟注赢 2 输 1，需要 33% 胜率才打平。如果你的范围恰好三分之一是诈唬，他跟与弃的期望相同——你变得无法被针对。
text.en: You bet pot on the river: a call risks 1 to win 2, needing 33% to break even. If exactly one-third of your range is bluffing, his call and his fold have equal expectation — there is nothing to aim at.

text.zh: 常见错误：某条线里只有坚果。会观察的对手很快学会对你这条线安全弃牌——你的大牌从此再也收不到钱。
text.en: Common mistake: a line that contains only the nuts. Observant opponents quickly learn to fold safely against it — and your monsters never get paid again.

text.zh: 平衡是对会调整的对手的防御。对不观察、不调整的对手，直接剥削更值钱——这是下一课。
text.en: Balance is a defense against adapting opponents. Versus players who never observe or adjust, straightforward exploitation earns more — that is the next lesson.

text.zh: 剥削是有意偏离均衡去打击对手的具体漏洞：他弃牌太多就多诈唬，他什么都跟就只下价值。代价是你的策略也出现缺口——剥削别人的人，同时把自己暴露给反剥削。
text.en: Exploitation means deliberately leaving equilibrium to attack a specific leak: bluff more against over-folders, value-bet only against stations. The cost is that your own strategy now has holes — the exploiter is simultaneously exposed to counter-exploitation.

text.zh: 面对 Calling Station（什么都跟、几乎不加注）：砍掉几乎全部诈唬，把薄价值下得更厚、更大。他用第二对跟你三条街，正是你利润的来源。
text.en: Versus a calling station (calls everything, almost never raises): cut nearly all bluffs and bet thinner value, bigger. His second pair calling three streets is exactly where your profit lives.

text.zh: 常见错误：对未知对手或强对手乱剥削。没有证据的偏离不是剥削，是漏洞；强对手会注意到并反打你。
text.en: Common mistake: 'exploiting' unknown or strong opponents. Deviation without evidence is not exploitation, it is a leak — and strong players will notice and punch back.

text.zh: 口诀：对未知，打基线；对已读懂的漏洞，做有方向的偏离；对手开始调整，就退回基线。
text.en: Mantra: unknown → baseline; a confirmed leak → deviate with direction; the moment they adjust → step back to baseline.

text.zh: 位置是翻后每条街都重复支付的复利：后行动意味着先看到对方的决定再做自己的——信息更多、控池更稳、诈唬更准、薄价值更敢下。同一手牌，在 BTN 的价值远高于在 UTG。
text.en: Position is interest that compounds on every postflop street: acting last means seeing his decision before making yours — more information, steadier pot control, sharper bluffs, braver thin value. The same hand is worth far more on the BTN than under the gun.

text.zh: 本应用的开局表把这件事画了出来：UTG 只开 13.7% 的牌，BTN 却开 41.5%——三倍的差距，全部来自位置让边缘牌得以实现权益。
text.en: This app's opening charts draw the picture: UTG opens just 13.7% of hands while the BTN opens 41.5% — a threefold gap, all because position lets marginal hands realize their equity.

text.zh: 常见错误：在没位置时玩投机牌「赌一把翻牌」。这类牌的利润恰恰依赖位置——换到 UTG 与盲注位，它们从资产变成负债。
text.en: Common mistake: playing speculative hands out of position 'to gamble on a flop.' Their profit depends precisely on position — moved to UTG or the blinds, they flip from asset to liability.

text.zh: 简单可靠的修正：位置每差一档，起手标准就收紧一档。
text.en: A simple, reliable fix: for every step worse your position, tighten your starting standard one notch.

text.zh: 翻前范围的形状，决定了你在每类牌面上的处境：紧而高张的范围在 A/K 高面上拥有天然范围优势；松而连张的范围在中低连面上更密合。翻后的难易，多半在翻前就写好了。
text.en: The shape of your preflop range decides your standing on every board class: tight, high-card ranges own A/K-high boards; loose, connected ranges mesh with middling connected ones. How hard postflop feels was mostly written preflop.

text.zh: UTG 开局范围（大对子 + 大高张）来到 A♥K♦6♣：几乎每个组合都命中或压制——可以放心高频施压。同一范围掉进 7♠6♠5♦，优势立刻易主。
text.en: The UTG opening range (big pairs plus big broadways) arrives on A♥K♦6♣: nearly every combo connects or dominates — press with confidence. Drop the same range onto 7♠6♠5♦ and the advantage changes hands instantly.

text.zh: 常见错误：翻前用杂牌乱跟，把自己的范围搅成一团浑水——翻后没有任何一类牌面真正属于你，每条街都在逆风做题。
text.en: Common mistake: splashing preflop with junk until your range is mud — no board class truly belongs to you, and every street becomes an uphill quiz.

text.zh: 翻前纪律的真正回报在翻后：范围干净的人，翻后的决策树也干净。
text.en: The real payoff of preflop discipline arrives postflop: a clean range makes a clean decision tree.

text.zh: 进步来自闭环而不是堆量：学一个概念 → 在训练场用题目验证 → 错题进入间隔复习 → 在数据页确认正确率回升。四步缺一，知识就会从指缝漏走。
text.en: Improvement comes from a closed loop, not raw volume: learn a concept → verify it in drills → send mistakes into spaced review → confirm on the stats page that accuracy recovers. Remove any step and knowledge leaks through your fingers.

text.zh: 本应用的错题本按 1 → 3 → 7 → 14 天安排复习——恰好卡在遗忘临界点之前重练。连续答对四个阶段，这道题才算真正「掌握」。
text.en: This app's mistake book schedules reviews at 1 → 3 → 7 → 14 days — re-drilling just before the forgetting cliff. Only after passing all four stages is a question truly 'mastered.'

text.zh: 常见错误：只刷新题、从不复盘错因。错题是你个人漏洞的精确地图，跳过它等于把地图扔掉重新迷路。
text.en: Common mistake: grinding new questions while never revisiting errors. Your mistakes are a precise map of your personal leaks; skipping review throws the map away and gets lost again.

text.zh: 小而稳的节奏赢过偶尔的爆肝：每天十分钟，先清到期复习，再开新训练。
text.en: Small and steady beats occasional binges: ten minutes a day — clear due reviews first, then start new drills.

prompt.zh: GTO 策略最核心的特征是什么？
prompt.en: What is the defining property of a GTO strategy?

choices.zh: 对每个对手都赢得最多
choices.en: It wins the most against every opponent

choices.zh: 不可被剥削——对手偏离也无法长期占便宜
choices.en: It is unexploitable — deviations cannot profit against it long-run

choices.zh: 永远选择最激进的动作
choices.en: It always picks the most aggressive action

choiceExplanations.zh: 赢得最多靠的是针对性剥削；GTO 提供的是不被针对的基线。
choiceExplanations.en: Winning the most takes targeted exploitation; GTO provides the un-targetable baseline.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：均衡的意义就是「无论你怎么偏离，都拿我没办法」。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: equilibrium means 'however you deviate, you cannot hurt me.'

choiceExplanations.zh: 均衡里既有激进也有克制——频率服从结构，不服从情绪。
choiceExplanations.en: Equilibrium contains both aggression and restraint — frequencies follow structure, not mood.

objective.zh: 用一句话说清 GTO 的本质。
objective.en: State the essence of GTO in one line.

prompt.zh: 石头剪刀布的均衡策略是什么？
prompt.en: What is the equilibrium strategy in rock-paper-scissors?

choices.zh: 三种手势各三分之一，完全随机
choices.en: One-third each, fully random

choices.zh: 永远出石头，保持强硬
choices.en: Always rock, stay firm

choices.zh: 模仿对手的上一手
choices.en: Copy the opponent's last throw

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：均匀随机让任何对策的期望都归零——这就是不可被剥削。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: uniform randomness zeroes out every counter-strategy — that is unexploitability.

choiceExplanations.zh: 固定出石头会被「永远出布」打到怀疑人生。
choiceExplanations.en: Pure rock loses endlessly to pure paper.

choiceExplanations.zh: 任何可预测的模式都能被反制——包括模仿。
choiceExplanations.en: Any predictable pattern can be countered — imitation included.

objective.zh: 用最小博弈理解均衡与随机化。
objective.en: Grasp equilibrium and randomization in the smallest game.

choices.zh: 猜出对手具体的两张牌
choices.en: Guessing the opponent's exact two cards

choices.zh: 只考虑自己手牌的强度
choices.en: Considering only your own hand strength

choices.zh: 按对手全部可能组合的分布来推理与决策
choices.en: Reasoning over the full distribution of combos the opponent can hold

choiceExplanations.zh: 单点猜测一旦落空，后续推理全部坍塌。
choiceExplanations.en: A pinpoint guess that misses collapses every later inference.

choiceExplanations.zh: 只看自己，等于闭着一只眼打牌。
choiceExplanations.en: Watching only your own cards is playing with one eye shut.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：动作过滤范围、牌面切割范围——决策对着整团分布做。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: actions filter ranges, boards carve them — decide against the whole cloud.

objective.zh: 从单手思维切换到范围思维。
objective.en: Switch from hand thinking to range thinking.

prompt.zh: 按本应用的图表，UTG 开局范围大约占全部起手牌的多少？
prompt.en: Per this app's charts, the UTG opening range covers roughly what share of all starting hands?

choices.en: About 5%
About 14%
About 30%

choiceExplanations.zh: 5% 几乎只剩超强对子——比 UTG 的实际口径紧得多。
choiceExplanations.en: Five percent is nearly premium-pairs-only — far tighter than UTG actually plays.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：182 个组合 ≈ 13.7%——六人桌最紧的开局位。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: 182 combos ≈ 13.7% — the tightest opening seat at six-max.

choiceExplanations.zh: 30% 已接近 CO 的口径，UTG 前面还有四个人等着反击。
choiceExplanations.en: Thirty percent is closer to the CO; UTG still has four players waiting to fight back.

objective.zh: 记住 UTG ≈ 14% 这个锚点数字。

prompt.zh: 混合策略（同一手牌按比例选不同动作）的目的为何？
prompt.en: Why mix actions (play the same hand different ways by ratio)?

choices.zh: 让对手无法从动作反推手牌，保持平衡
choices.en: So opponents cannot infer the hand from the action — staying balanced

choices.zh: 给牌局增加趣味
choices.en: To make the game more fun

choices.zh: 掩饰自己的犹豫
choices.en: To hide indecision

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：混合让每条线里强弱共存，固定模式才是被读穿的根源。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: mixing keeps strength and bluffs together on every line; fixed patterns are what get read.

choiceExplanations.zh: 趣味是副产品，平衡才是目的。
choiceExplanations.en: Fun is a byproduct; balance is the purpose.

choiceExplanations.zh: 均衡里的随机是设计出来的纪律，不是犹豫。
choiceExplanations.en: Equilibrium randomness is designed discipline, not hesitation.

objective.zh: 理解混合策略的防御价值。

prompt.zh: 训练判分里的「可接受」评级，最准确的含义是？
prompt.en: In drill grading, the 'acceptable' rating most precisely means?

choices.zh: 答错了，但给个安慰分
choices.en: Wrong, but here is a consolation point

choices.zh: 它是混合策略中的另一翼合理选项
choices.en: It is the other legitimate wing of a mixed strategy

choices.zh: 系统无法判断对错
choices.en: The system cannot tell right from wrong

choiceExplanations.zh: 「可接受」不是错——它在真实均衡里确实以一定频率出现。
choiceExplanations.en: 'Acceptable' is not wrong — it genuinely occurs at some frequency in equilibrium.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：主答案是主频动作，可接受是同一均衡里的次频动作。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: the main answer is the majority action; acceptable is the minority action of the same equilibrium.

choiceExplanations.zh: 判分口径来自图表频率，清晰且可解释。
choiceExplanations.en: Grading follows chart frequencies — explicit and explainable.

prompt.zh: 牌面三张黑桃，你持 A♠ 但没有同花。这张 A♠ 为什么提升你的诈唬？
prompt.en: Three spades on board and you hold the A♠ without a flush. Why does that card upgrade your bluff?

choices.zh: 它阻断了坚果同花，对手最强的跟注理由变少
choices.en: It blocks the nut flush — his best calling hands got rarer

choices.zh: A 是最大的牌，自带威慑
choices.en: An ace is the biggest card and intimidates

choices.zh: 它保证下一张还能中同花
choices.en: It promises another spade is coming

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：对手拿坚果同花的组合被你掐掉了一批——他更难用强牌跟注。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: you have removed a chunk of his nut-flush combos — strong calls just got scarcer.

choiceExplanations.zh: 威慑是心理叙事；阻断是组合数学。
choiceExplanations.en: Intimidation is a story; blocking is combinatorics.

choiceExplanations.zh: 阻断改变的是对手现在的组合分布，不预言未来的牌。
choiceExplanations.en: Blockers reshape his current combo distribution; they predict no future cards.

objective.zh: 用组合数解释经典的 A♠ 阻断。
objective.en: Explain the classic A♠ block with combos.

prompt.zh: 阻断牌发挥作用的机制是什么？
prompt.en: Through what mechanism do blockers work?

choices.zh: 提升你自己的成牌概率
choices.en: They raise your own chance of making a hand

choices.zh: 减少对手范围里特定组合的数量
choices.en: They reduce specific combo counts inside the opponent's range

choices.zh: 改变底池赔率
choices.en: They change the pot odds

choiceExplanations.zh: 你的成牌概率由自己的听牌结构决定，与阻断无关。
choiceExplanations.en: Your own hand-making odds come from your draw structure, not blockers.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：你持有的牌，对手就拿不到——一张 A 让 AA 从 6 组降到 3 组。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: cards in your hand cannot be in his — one ace cuts AA from 6 combos to 3.

choiceExplanations.zh: 赔率由注码与底池决定；阻断改变的是范围构成。
choiceExplanations.en: Odds come from bet and pot size; blockers reshape range composition.

objective.zh: 说清阻断效应的组合学原理。
objective.en: State the combinatorial principle of blocking.

prompt.zh: 河牌做底池大小的下注，均衡状态下诈唬大约占多少？
prompt.en: Betting pot on the river, what share of the range bluffs at equilibrium?

choices.zh: 约三分之二
choices.en: About two-thirds

choices.zh: 约一半
choices.en: About half

choices.zh: 约三分之一
choices.en: About one-third

choiceExplanations.zh: 诈唬到三分之二，对手闭眼跟注都有得赚。
choiceExplanations.en: At two-thirds bluffs, blind calls print against you.

choiceExplanations.zh: 一半仍然超过这个尺寸允许的密度。
choiceExplanations.en: Half is still denser than this sizing supports.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：对手跟注需 33% 胜率，诈唬占比 1/3 让他跟与弃无差——无懈可击。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: a pot-odds call needs 33%, and one-third bluffs makes call and fold equal — nothing to attack.

objective.zh: 把尺寸与诈唬密度挂钩。

prompt.zh: 如果你的某条下注线里永远只有坚果，长期会发生什么？
prompt.en: If one of your betting lines only ever contains the nuts, what happens long-term?

choices.zh: 会观察的对手对这条线安全弃牌，你的大牌再也收不到钱
choices.en: Observant opponents fold safely to it, and your monsters stop getting paid

choices.zh: 你变得更难被击败
choices.en: You become harder to beat

choices.zh: 毫无影响
choices.en: Nothing changes

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：可读的强即是弱——没人付钱的坚果只是漂亮的摆设。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: readable strength is weakness — unpaid nuts are just decoration.

choiceExplanations.zh: 这条线确实「输不了」，却也赢不到——期望被弃牌抽干。
choiceExplanations.en: The line cannot lose, but it also cannot earn — folds drain its expectation.

choiceExplanations.zh: 对会调整的对手，任何固定模式都会被定价。
choiceExplanations.en: Against adapting players, every fixed pattern gets priced in.

objective.zh: 理解「只有坚果的线」为何被弃牌剥削。
objective.en: See why nuts-only lines get exploited by folding.

prompt.zh: 面对典型 Calling Station（什么都跟、几乎不加注），最直接的调整是？
prompt.en: Versus a textbook calling station (calls everything, rarely raises), the most direct adjustment is?

choices.zh: 增加诈唬频率压垮他
choices.en: Bluff more to overwhelm him

choices.zh: 加厚价值下注，删减几乎全部诈唬
choices.en: Bet thinner value bigger and cut nearly all bluffs

choices.zh: 严格维持均衡频率
choices.en: Hold exact equilibrium frequencies

choiceExplanations.zh: 对不弃牌的人诈唬，是把筹码直接递过去。
choiceExplanations.en: Bluffing someone who never folds hands him your chips.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：他付钱看牌的习惯，正好用更厚、更大的价值去收。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: his pay-to-see habit is harvested with thicker, bigger value.

choiceExplanations.zh: 均衡防的是会调整的对手；对不调整的漏洞，偏离才是利润。
choiceExplanations.en: Equilibrium guards against adapters; versus a static leak, deviation is the profit.

objective.zh: 对跟注站执行教科书剥削。

prompt.zh: 什么时候应该回到 GTO 基线打法？
prompt.en: When should you return to the GTO baseline?

choices.zh: 永远不需要——剥削总是更好
choices.en: Never — exploitation is always better

choices.zh: 只在拿到强牌时
choices.en: Only when dealt strong hands

choices.zh: 对手未知，或对手已开始针对你的偏离时
choices.en: When opponents are unknown, or have begun countering your deviation

choiceExplanations.zh: 剥削依赖证据；没有读数的偏离只是自己的漏洞。
choiceExplanations.en: Exploitation runs on evidence; deviation without reads is just your own leak.

choiceExplanations.zh: 基线是整套策略框架，与某一手牌的强弱无关。
choiceExplanations.en: The baseline is a whole framework, independent of any single hand.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：「未知打基线，被反制就回家」——基线是进可攻退可守的大本营。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: unknown → baseline, countered → come home; the baseline is base camp.

objective.zh: 掌握剥削与回归的切换时机。
objective.en: Time the switch between exploiting and returning.

prompt.zh: 位置优势的本质是什么？
prompt.en: What is the essence of positional advantage?

choices.zh: 后行动：先看到对手决定再做自己的
choices.en: Acting last: seeing his decision before making yours

choices.zh: 离庄家按钮更近，气场更强
choices.en: Sitting near the button radiates strength

choices.zh: 能拿到更好的起手牌
choices.en: Being dealt better starting hands

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：信息差在每条街复利——控池、诈唬、薄价值全部因此变准。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: the information edge compounds every street — pot control, bluffs and thin value all sharpen.

choiceExplanations.zh: 气场不进底池；信息才进。
choiceExplanations.en: Aura does not enter the pot; information does.

choiceExplanations.zh: 发牌对每个座位一视同仁，位置改变的是牌的「可实现价值」。
choiceExplanations.en: Cards fall evenly on every seat; position changes what the cards can realize.

objective.zh: 把位置优势归因到信息顺序。

prompt.zh: 为什么 BTN 能开 41.5% 的牌，而 UTG 只开 13.7%？
prompt.en: Why can the BTN open 41.5% of hands while UTG opens only 13.7%?

choices.zh: BTN 的运气传统上更好
choices.en: The BTN is traditionally luckier

choices.zh: 位置让大量边缘牌得以实现权益
choices.en: Position lets a mass of marginal hands realize their equity

choices.zh: 规则要求 BTN 多开牌
choices.en: The rules require the BTN to open more

choiceExplanations.zh: 没有「位置运」这回事——只有位置期望。
choiceExplanations.en: There is no positional luck — only positional expectation.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：同一手 T8s，在 BTN 是资产、在 UTG 是负债；三倍差距全是位置的复利。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: the same T8s is an asset on the BTN and a liability under the gun; the threefold gap is position compounding.

choiceExplanations.zh: 规则只规定行动顺序；开局宽窄是策略对顺序的回应。
choiceExplanations.en: Rules fix the order of action; range width is strategy's answer to that order.

objective.zh: 用实现权益解释 41.5% 对 13.7%。

prompt.zh: 翻前范围的形状，决定了翻后的什么？
prompt.en: The shape of a preflop range decides what, postflop?

choices.zh: 公共牌会发出哪些牌
choices.en: Which community cards will fall

choices.zh: 底池最终的大小
choices.en: The final size of the pot

choices.zh: 你在各类牌面上的范围优势归属
choices.en: Who owns range advantage on each board class

choiceExplanations.zh: 发什么牌是随机的；范围决定的是你与每类牌面的关系。
choiceExplanations.en: Which cards fall is random; ranges decide your relationship to each board class.

choiceExplanations.zh: 底池大小由下注树决定，那是结果不是原因。
choiceExplanations.en: Pot size follows the betting tree — an effect, not the cause.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：紧高张范围拥有 A/K 高面，松连张范围拥有中低连面——版图翻前已划定。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: tight high-card ranges own A/K-high boards, loose connected ranges own middling ones — the map is drawn preflop.

objective.zh: 把翻后优势追溯到翻前范围。
objective.en: Trace postflop advantage back to preflop range.

prompt.zh: UTG 的开局范围（大对子 + 大高张）最舒服的翻牌是哪类？
prompt.en: Which flop class is most comfortable for the UTG opening range (big pairs plus big broadways)?

choices.zh: A/K 高的干燥牌面
choices.en: Ace- or king-high dry boards

choices.zh: 765 这类中低连面
choices.en: Low connected boards like 765

choices.zh: 所有牌面都一样
choices.en: Every board feels the same

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：AK、AQ、KQ 与大对子在这里密集命中或压制。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: AK, AQ, KQ and big pairs land densely or dominate here.

choiceExplanations.zh: 中低连面正撞防守方的同花连张——是 UTG 范围最落空的版图。
choiceExplanations.en: Low connected boards crash into the defender's suited connectors — UTG's biggest whiff zone.

choiceExplanations.zh: 范围与牌面的密合度天差地别，「都一样」恰是单手思维的症状。
choiceExplanations.en: Range-board mesh varies wildly; 'all the same' is the symptom of hand-only thinking.

objective.zh: 识别紧范围的主场牌面。
objective.en: Identify a tight range's home turf.

prompt.zh: 错题按 1 → 3 → 7 → 14 天间隔复习，价值在哪里？
prompt.en: Reviews at 1 → 3 → 7 → 14 day intervals — what is the value?

choices.zh: 把复习拖到最后一刻更省事
choices.en: Procrastinating reviews is simply easier

choices.zh: 在遗忘临界点之前重练，把短期记忆固化为长期
choices.en: Re-drilling just before the forgetting cliff turns short-term recall into long-term

choices.zh: 间隔本身没有意义，只是排版好看
choices.en: The spacing is meaningless decoration

choiceExplanations.zh: 拖延复习等于每次都从零重学。
choiceExplanations.en: Procrastinated review means relearning from zero each time.

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：间隔重复是对抗遗忘曲线最被验证的武器。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: spaced repetition is the best-proven weapon against the forgetting curve.

choiceExplanations.zh: 递增的间隔正对应记忆衰减的节奏——它是科学，不是排版。
choiceExplanations.en: The widening gaps track memory decay — science, not typesetting.

objective.zh: 理解 SRS 间隔背后的记忆原理。
objective.en: Understand the memory science behind SRS intervals.

prompt.zh: 本应用建议的完整训练循环是？
prompt.en: What is the full training loop this app recommends?

choices.zh: 学课程 → 练场景 → 复习错题 → 看统计复盘
choices.en: Learn lessons → drill scenarios → review mistakes → check stats

choices.zh: 只刷无尽模式，量大出奇迹
choices.en: Grind endless mode only — volume cures all

choices.zh: 把全部课程读完再开始做题
choices.en: Read every lesson before attempting any drill

choiceExplanations.zh: 正确：学、练、复习、复盘四步闭环，缺一处知识就漏一处。
choiceExplanations.en: Correct: learn, drill, review, reflect — a closed loop that leaks wherever a step is missing.

choiceExplanations.zh: 没有复盘的量只是重复，重复同样的漏洞。
choiceExplanations.en: Volume without reflection is repetition — of the same leaks.

choiceExplanations.zh: 概念要趁热在题目里验证；攒到最后一起读，遗忘曲线先到。
choiceExplanations.en: Concepts want immediate testing in drills; saved-up reading loses to the forgetting curve.

objective.zh: 把四步循环变成日常习惯。
objective.en: Make the four-step loop a daily habit.
